philosophy.core: Code is read far more than it's written. Optimize for the reader, not the writer.
philosophy.goal: Ship code that a junior developer can understand in 30 seconds. If they can't, refactor.

naming.test: If someone reads this name 6 months from now, will they understand what it does without reading the implementation?
naming.standard: Names express intent. No abbreviations. No mental mapping required.

functions.singleResponsibility: Each function does ONE thing and does it well. If you need 'and' to describe what it does, extract.

parameters.guidance: Red flag - function likely does multiple things

functions.standard: Each function does ONE thing. <20 lines ideal. Extract when >30.

file-limits.godFileRule: Any file >500 lines is a God File. Split immediately, no exceptions.

error-handling.rule: No silent failures - always provide user feedback.
requirements: Visible feedback for both success AND failure
Structured returns (Result types, Error boundaries, or explicit error states)
Clear messages - actionable, user-friendly, not technical jargon
Development logs only - never swallow errors silently in production
error-handling.standard: No silent failures. Users see feedback. Structured error returns.

anti-patterns.fix: Remove completely - version control is your backup
Add UI feedback for all errors

dontDocument: What code does (if you need to explain, refactor the code)
documentation.test: Is this comment explaining WHY (keep it) or WHAT (delete it and make the code clearer instead)?
documentation.standard: JSDoc for public APIs. Design notes for WHY. No apologetic comments.

refactoring-checklist.action: All catch blocks have UI feedback
No repeated expensive operations in render
Numbers/strings used multiple times are named
Utils for functions, types for shapes

solid.core: Open for extension, closed for modification
solid.testQuestion: Can I add features without changing existing code?
Can I swap implementations without breaking?

solid.core: No client depends on unused methods
solid.testQuestion: Does this interface have methods I don't need?
Am I depending on an interface or implementation?

threeQuestions.question: Can a junior dev understand this in 30 seconds?
Am I solving today's problem or imaginary futures?
threeQuestions.ifFuture: Stop and solve only what's needed

threeQuestions.question: Would deleting this make the system simpler?

gradingRubric.standard: Names express intent. No abbreviations. No mental mapping required.
Each function does ONE thing. <20 lines ideal. Extract when >30.
JSDoc for public APIs. Design notes for WHY. No apologetic comments.
Logical top-to-bottom flow. Helpers first, then main component.
No silent failures. Users see feedback. Structured error returns.
No magic numbers. No duplication. No dead code.
Single Responsibility. Open for extension. Clear abstractions.

source: Uncle Bob's Clean Code, adapted for modern development
